methodology: Joint outlier score (joint neutral-draw tail probability), canonical ensemble
interpretation: P(feature vector | neutral draw). NOT a posterior probability of gerrymandering — no prior is specified. Low values mean the neutral null is implausible as an explanation for the observed feature vector.

szat.bootstrap_p_label: 0.0024 (24/10000 permutations exceeded observed, full-recompute, full advance-vote substrate)
szat.bootstrap_procedure: full-recompute (pre-registered): each swing-zone VA Bernoulli-assigned to majority_ed or minority_ed; EG recomputed from scratch per permutation; SZAT_perm = EG(perm_minority) - EG(majority_fixed)

municipal_anchoring.status: RETRACTED — canonical shapefile recomputation placed both 2026 maps within the Canadian comparator norm; departure_factor finding did not survive. DPG-era values were 14.5% (minority) / 71.0% (majority); superseded by canonical geometry. See analysis/methodology/canonical_shapefile_log.md.
municipal_anchoring.p_value: not computed — finding retracted on canonical geometry

population_mad_ratio.p_value: pending — per-plan MAD not in ensemble outputs

reock_asymmetry.p_value: pending — per-plan Reock not in ensemble outputs

neighbour_drain.note: minority within null (p=0.1342); majority anomalously low (p<0.0001). Channel not included in Fisher combination.

caveats: Ensemble is 1,010,000 plans (canonical shapefiles, 4 chains x 252,500); n_eff ~1,429-1,682 per metric.
Replaces DPG-based 250k ensemble and previous 100k canonical run; canonical shapefiles are official Elections Alberta files.
Marginal tail probabilities (per-metric percentiles) carried from prior canonical pass; joint D² and Fisher p recomputed from full 1M covariance matrix.
Fisher combination assumes Channel 1 and Channel 2 are independent — approximately true.
Mahalanobis assumes multivariate Gaussian ensemble distribution — informally verified.
This score answers P(features | neutral), not P(gerrymandered | features).
